title: Illegal Alien Child Killer Walked Free Under Biden — Trump Admin Locks Door For Good
author: Luke Rosiak
fullText: An illegal immigrant whom the Biden administration did not deport after he served just three years in prison after killing an 8-year-old boy is now in Immigration and Customs Enforcement custody, The Daily Wire has learned.In 2019, Karmjit Singh was driving drunk in his BMW sports car when he hit another vehicle at more than 100 miles per hour, then tried to flee the scene. The crash killed Maverick Martzen, an 8-year-old star baseball player, and severely injured his parents, Scott and Megan.“I thought it was 25 years to life, easy case. He killed an 8-year-old,” Scott Martzen said. But Singh — an Indian national who had overstayed his visa and already had a separate felony DUI conviction — served just three years in prison.The term didn’t teach him his lesson: After he was released, he got two more DUIs.In June 2023, he was arrested for driving drunk at 2 a.m. and giving police his brother Parminder’s driver’s license. Police ascertained his real identity and that his license was suspended for manslaughter, but released him anyway. In November 2023, the same thing happened.He went back to prison for another short term, and on January 30, he was released by California, Scott Martzen told The Daily Wire.“Despite an ICE hold, he has once again been released on parole,” he said. Martzen did not receive any notification from authorities, but noticed on the California prison system’s website that Singh had been released on parole, and believed he was free again.“Second chance. Third chance. Fourth chance. Fifth chance. Our son never got one more day,” he told The Daily Wire.ICE did not return a request for comment earlier this month, but a search of ICE’s detainee database using Singh’s name and birthday shows that he is now being held at the California City Corrections Center, a former jail that was reopened in 2026 as a massive ICE holding facility, part of the numerous warehouses that the agency is opening across the country.That center is the largest immigration facility in California and can hold up to 2,500 people.“Every birthday, every holiday, every baseball season, we live with the reality that Maverick is gone,” Scott Martzen told The Daily Wire. “Our younger son is growing up without his big brother because one man chose to drink and drive, and a system keeps giving him chance after chance.”The grieving father previously told The Daily Wire that Maverick would “100%” be alive today if the government had deported Singh after his first felony conviction in 2016. He said the country is “not at all” better for having Singh here, in contrast to the promise shown by Maverick, a gifted athlete, advanced student, and godly son, he said.“What does it take” to be deported, he asked in 2024. “He killed a kid, he’s got an expired visa, no drivers license, two DUIs, a third that should have been a DUI but was lowered, and he’s still able to live here … it’s beyond frustrating.”To honor the gifted athlete, who was the first draft pick for a baseball team of 12-year-olds when he was 8, the family started a nonprofit called M30 to help child athletes. While Major League Baseball played with no fans in the stands during COVID, a cardboard cutout of Maverick occupied a seat at the San Francisco Giants’ stadium.
summary: An illegal immigrant who killed an eight-year old American, but went to prison for only three years in California, and was not deported upon his release by the Joe Biden administration, is now in ICE custody.
